edge_hypothesis: Every strategy in the system uses a fixed 1.5x ATR stop and varies only the TP multiplier to change R:R. But stop width is an independent variable that has NEVER been tested. A tighter stop (1.0x ATR) exits losing trades faster, reducing time-in-drawdown and capital exposure per trade. A wider stop (2.0x ATR) gives trades more room to breathe, potentially reducing stop-outs from noise. When you change stop width while maintaining the SAME R:R ratio (by proportionally adjusting TP), you test a fundamentally different question: does the distance-to-stop affect PF independently of R:R? On the MACD 4h template, peak R:R is 7:1 with 1.5x/10.5x stop/TP. This spec tests: (1) 1.0x stop / 7.0x TP = still 7:1 R:R but tighter initial risk, (2) 2.0x stop / 14.0x TP = still 7:1 R:R but wider breathing room. If tighter stops improve PF, the 1.5x ATR standard was leaving edge on the table. If wider stops improve PF, noise-induced stop-outs were a drag on the 1.5x standard. Additionally, tests 1h MACD with tight stop (1.0x/7.0x) — on 1h, ATR is smaller in absolute terms, so a tight 1.0x ATR stop may be proportionally tighter than on 4h, filtering entries that move against immediately. This is independent, novel research that applies to ALL templates once the stop-width sensitivity is characterized. Invalidation: if all variants produce PF within 5% of the 1.5x baseline, stop width doesn't matter and 1.5x is fine.

variants.name: macd_tight_stop_4h_7to1
variants.description: MACD confirmation on ETH/4h with TIGHT stop: 1.0x ATR stop, 7.0x ATR TP. R:R = 7:1 (same as proven peak). This is the key test: does a tighter stop improve the PF=1.712 champion? With a 1.0x ATR stop, losing trades exit 33% faster than the 1.5x standard. If noise causes frequent stop-outs, PF will degrade (tighter stop = more losers). If the MACD signal is clean and losers tend to move against immediately, PF will improve (fewer losers drag on for 1.5x before stopping). Expected: 160-170 trades (same entry signal, so same trade count as 7:1 baseline), PF range 1.4-1.9 depending on stop sensitivity.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses above zero (histogram turns positive from negative).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses below zero (histogram turns negative from positive).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
ADX regime gate: ADX_14 > 15 (trending or strong ranging)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (7.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 7:1 (same as proven peak, but with TIGHT stop).
Stop width study: 1.0x ATR vs 1.5x standard — does tighter stop improve the MACD champion?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend confirmation — same as standard MACD
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-line cross — same entry as champion, testing stop width only
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 7.0x TP — TIGHT stop, same 7:1 R:R as champion, isolating stop width impact

variants.name: macd_wide_stop_4h_7to1
variants.description: MACD confirmation on ETH/4h with WIDE stop: 2.0x ATR stop, 14.0x ATR TP. R:R = 7:1 (same as proven peak). The opposite extreme: gives trades 33% more breathing room than the 1.5x standard. If the MACD signal is fundamentally correct but price noise causes 1.5x stop-outs before the trade works, a wider stop should improve win rate. The wider TP (14.0x ATR) is extreme — only the strongest trends will hit it. This may shift the strategy to 'trending-only' mode (fewer but larger winners). Expected: 160-170 trades (same entries), but potentially different win rate and regime distribution than the tight/standard variants.

variants.name: macd_tight_stop_1h_7to1
variants.description: MACD confirmation on ETH/1h with TIGHT stop: 1.0x ATR stop, 7.0x ATR TP. R:R = 7:1. On 1h, ATR_14 represents ~14 hours of volatility (vs ~56 hours on 4h). A 1.0x ATR stop on 1h is proportionally tighter in absolute dollar terms. This creates a 'hair-trigger' exit: if the trade moves 1 ATR against within the first few bars, it's stopped out. The hypothesis is that on 1h, the MACD histogram zero-line cross provides enough momentum that winners move in the right direction immediately — losers that hesitate should be cut fast. This is the most aggressive stop configuration tested on any template. Expected: 150-170 trades, but potentially higher win-rate on winners (strong momentum), with more stop-outs on marginal trades.

entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses below zero (histogram turns negative from positive).

Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Tight 1h stop: hair-trigger exit on wrong trades, maximizing capital efficiency.
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 7.0x ATR_14 from entry.
